wherefore
Brand & Identity Style Guide

The system of record for how wherefore looks, reads, and ships.

A single reference for building the product UI, the docs site, the dashboard, and anything carrying the mark — and for prompting an AI to generate on-brand work. v1.0 · locked direction.

00 — Essence
What it is

An open, inspectable, dead-simple record of why — the reasoning, tradeoffs, and rejected alternatives behind engineering decisions, stored as plain markdown in your own repo.

How it should feel

Serious infrastructure for engineers. Calm, sharp, understated — a little vintage and literate. Cut to what works; restraint over decoration.

flat & precise technical literate open / inspectable not playful · not consumer-y
01 — Logo system
wherefore

The primary lockup: the ∵ icon (the “because” operator) left of the wordmark, vertically centered. Icon height ≈ cap height of the word; gap ≈ 0.4× icon width. No prompt caret, no cursor, no other ornament.

Icon construction (∵)
viewBox 0 0 100 100
r 13
top-L 34, 34
top-R 66, 34
bottom 50, 66
three equal circles —
geometry, never a glyph
Clear space & min size

Keep clear space ≥ the icon’s width on all sides. Don’t crowd the lockup with UI.

  • Full lockup min: 120px wide
  • Wordmark alone min: 88px wide
  • Below that, use the ∵ icon alone
icon
wherefore wordmark
wherefore
on light
02 — Color
Charcoal — ground
#14151a
surface #191a20 · #1a1a22
Teal — accent / dots
#4ab5b7 bright
base #3a9b9d
Teal — on light
#258a84
use on white surfaces
Ink — text
#f0eadc
on-light text #181a1f
Muted — meta
#6a6a72
labels, captions
True black — tile
#0f1014
favicon tile option

Teal is a signal, not a fill — reserve it for the dots, links, focus states, and the rare key accent. One teal per view. Never tint large surfaces with it.

03 — Status colors

Decision states are ranked by attention, not alarm. Nothing is ever deleted — superseded and rejected entries stay in the record and simply recede. No saturated UI red. Teal is the only color that draws the eye; everything historical steps back.

Current
#4ab5b7 · teal
Proposed
#c9943f · amber
Superseded
#6a6a72 · muted
Rejected
#b06a5a · oxblood
Applied — decision-log entries
Use Postgres row-level security for tenant isolation
adr-0042 · 2026-05-18
current
Move background jobs to a dedicated queue service
adr-0051 · 2026-06-21 · under review
proposed
Isolate tenants with one schema per customer
adr-0019 · 2025-11-02 · ↳ superseded by adr-0042
superseded
Adopt a third-party cloud memory service
adr-0033 · 2026-02-14 · lock-in, no inspectability
rejected

Superseded rows dim to ~60% and strike the title; rejected rows dim slightly and carry the one-line reason. Only current keeps full ink and the teal signal.

On light surfaces  · darkened for legibility, same hierarchy
Current
#258a84
Proposed
#a8731f
Superseded
#8a8a90
Rejected
#9c4f3c
Use Postgres row-level security for tenant isolation
adr-0042 · 2026-05-18
current
Isolate tenants with one schema per customer
adr-0019 · 2025-11-02 · ↳ superseded by adr-0042
superseded
04 — Typography
Space Grotesk wordmark · headings · weight 500–600
The why behind your code.
wordmark: lowercase · 600 · letter-spacing -0.02em
Inter UI / body

Product UI, prose, and long-form documentation. Regular and medium; keep line length comfortable and contrast high.

IBM Plex Mono code / labels

decision.md · metadata · eyebrow labels
$ wherefore log  # the why, in your repo

Note: the wordmark is locked to Space Grotesk. Earlier JetBrains-Mono explorations are superseded by this direction.

05 — Voice & taglines
The why behind your code.
primary tagline
Not where. Why.
short / punchy
Do
  • Precise, plain, declarative
  • Speak to engineers as peers
  • Lean on the “why = wherefore” idea
  • Let whitespace and restraint carry it
Don't
  • Neon glow, scanlines, CRT effects
  • Drop shadows, gradients, 3D, purple
  • Condensed display fonts, emoji
  • Playful or consumer-y marketing tone
06 — Tokens & snippets
CSS custom properties
:root {
  --wf-ground:        #14151a;
  --wf-surface:       #191a20;
  --wf-teal:          #4ab5b7;  /* dots, accent */
  --wf-teal-base:     #3a9b9d;
  --wf-teal-on-light: #258a84;  /* on white */
  --wf-ink:           #f0eadc;
  --wf-ink-on-light:  #181a1f;
  --wf-muted:         #6a6a72;
  --wf-tile:          #0f1014;  /* favicon */
  /* status */
  --wf-current:       #4ab5b7;
  --wf-proposed:      #c9943f;
  --wf-superseded:    #6a6a72;
  --wf-rejected:      #b06a5a;
  /* status — on light */
  --wf-current-lt:    #258a84;
  --wf-proposed-lt:   #a8731f;
  --wf-superseded-lt: #8a8a90;
  --wf-rejected-lt:   #9c4f3c;
  --wf-font-display:  "Space Grotesk", sans-serif;
  --wf-font-ui:       "Inter", sans-serif;
  --wf-font-mono:     "IBM Plex Mono", monospace;
}
Favicon & head tags
<link rel="icon" href="/favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml">
<link rel="icon" href="/favicon-32.png" sizes="32x32">
<link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="/favicon-180.png">
Icon (∵) — inline SVG, geometry only
<svg viewBox="0 0 100 100">
  <circle cx="34" cy="34" r="13" fill="#4ab5b7"/>
  <circle cx="66" cy="34" r="13" fill="#4ab5b7"/>
  <circle cx="50" cy="66" r="13" fill="#4ab5b7"/>
</svg>
07 — Drop-in prompt block

Paste this at the top of any prompt to an AI builder to generate on-brand wherefore work.

wherefore-brand.prompt.txt 
Brand: wherefore — an open-source plugin that captures the WHY behind
engineering decisions as plain markdown in the user's own git repo.
Positioning: open, inspectable, dead-simple record of why. Serious
infrastructure for engineers — calm, sharp, understated, a little vintage
and literate. Philosophy: cut to what works; restraint over decoration.

VISUAL SYSTEM
- Wordmark: "wherefore", all lowercase, Space Grotesk 600, letter-spacing
  -0.02em.
- Icon: the "because" operator (three equal teal dots, two on top, one
  centered below). Always geometry, never a font glyph.
- Lockup: icon left of wordmark, centered; icon height = cap height;
  gap = 0.4x icon width.
- Type: Space Grotesk (display/headings), Inter (UI/body), IBM Plex Mono
  (code/labels).

COLOR
- Ground #14151a, surface #191a20, ink #f0eadc, muted #6a6a72.
- Teal accent #4ab5b7 (use #258a84 on light surfaces; ink #181a1f on light).
- Teal is a signal: dots, links, focus, one key accent per view. Never a fill.

STATUS (ranked by attention, never saturated red)
- current #4ab5b7 · proposed #c9943f · superseded #6a6a72 · rejected #b06a5a
- Nothing is deleted. Superseded/rejected entries recede (dimmed, struck);
  only the current decision keeps full ink and the teal signal.

RULES
- Flat and precise. NO gradients, glows, scanlines, CRT, drop shadows, 3D,
  purple, condensed display fonts, or emoji.
- Lowercase wordmark everywhere. Lots of negative space.
- Taglines: "The why behind your code." / "Not where. Why."
wherefore
style guide · v1.0
